user.title: Add Tion account
user.description: Sign in with the same Tion account used in the official app. Home Assistant will validate the credentials and load available locations, zones, and devices.

data_description.username: Tion account login, usually an email address.
data_description.password: Tion account password. It is stored in the integration settings so Home Assistant can refresh auth tokens.

reauth_confirm.title: Update Tion credentials
reauth_confirm.description: Home Assistant can no longer authenticate with Tion. Enter the current password for the already configured account; use Add integration to connect a different account.

data_description.password: Current password for this Tion account.

abort.already_configured: This Tion account is already configured.
abort.reauth_successful: Tion credentials were updated successfully. The integration has been reloaded.
abort.wrong_account: The password belongs to a different Tion account. Enter the password for the account already configured.

init.title: Tion options
init.description: Choose what to configure. Option changes are saved only when you return to this step and select Done.

data_description.scan_interval: Minimum time in seconds between Tion cloud API polls. Lower values refresh faster but make more cloud requests.
data_description.options_action: Open local PID settings, configure speed presets, or select Done to save all pending option changes.

local_pid.title: Local CO2 PID
local_pid.description: Local PID lets Home Assistant control one breezer from an external CO2 sensor. Select a breezer and an action; changes are applied after the final Done on the main options step.

data_description.breezer_guid: Breezer that should use an external Home Assistant CO2 sensor instead of Tion cloud auto control.
data_description.local_pid_action: Configure the selected breezer, remove its PID settings, or return without changing local PID settings.

breezer.title: Configure local PID
breezer.description: The default PID coefficients are recommended for typical setups. Change them only if you understand PID tuning. Saving this form returns to the local PID menu; use Done on the main options step to apply changes.

data_description.pid_enabled: When enabled, selecting Fan Auto on this breezer starts local PID control and keeps the Tion zone in manual mode.
data_description.co2_sensor_entity_id: Required when enabled. Select a Home Assistant sensor with CO2 device class and a numeric ppm value.
data_description.pid_base_output: Baseline output in percent before PID correction. Default: 20. Keep the default unless the breezer under-ventilates near the target.
data_description.pid_kp: Default: 0.5. Higher values react more strongly to the current CO2 error.
data_description.pid_ki: Default: 0.001. Integral gain accumulates long-running error; increase carefully to avoid overshoot.
data_description.pid_kd: Default: 0.0. Usually leave this at 0 because CO2 sensors often produce noisy derivative signals.

presets.title: Speed presets
presets.description: Configure speed presets separately for each breezer. Preset changes are applied after the final Done on the main options step.

data_description.breezer_guid: Breezer whose climate preset list should be changed.
data_description.presets_action: Add a new preset, edit speed values for an existing preset, remove a preset, or return to the main options step.

preset_add.title: Add preset
preset_add.description: Choose a Home Assistant preset name and how it should control this breezer. To change a preset type later, remove it and add it again.

data_description.preset_name: Preset name to expose on this breezer climate entity.
data_description.type: Auto stores minimum and maximum Auto-mode speeds; Manual stores one fixed fan speed.

preset_config.title: Configure preset {preset_name}
preset_config.description: Set the speeds for this preset. Available speed range is limited by the selected breezer model.

data_description.min_speed: Lower speed limit used when the Auto preset is active.
data_description.max_speed: Upper speed limit used when the Auto preset is active. It cannot be lower than the minimum speed.
data_description.speed: Fixed fan speed used when the Manual preset is active.

preset_edit.title: Edit preset
preset_edit.description: Select an existing preset to edit its speed values. The preset type cannot be changed here.

data_description.preset_name: Preset whose speed values should be edited.

preset_remove.title: Remove preset
preset_remove.description: Select a preset to remove from this breezer. Removal is saved after the final Done on the main options step.

data_description.preset_name: Preset that should be removed from this breezer.

error.min_above_max: Minimum speed must not be greater than maximum speed
error.all_presets_configured: All supported preset names are already configured for this breezer
